author: [AEP] WindSplitter1 [AEP]
content:
Fellow Atlassians, I have a couple of quick questions concerning the DAO. This is something I do not fully understand... Hopefully, a kind soul can bring me up to speed:

1 - From the econ paper, there are 3 DAOs pertaining to the 3 main factions. This is where player-driven decision-making will be held.
But as far as the Master DAO is concerned, it stands in control of Automata, correct?

2 - The point of the DAOs is governance. But from a practical standpoint (over decision making and creating a self-sustaining economy) does it serve a further purpose to the player? I can fully grasp the need for the Team to vector most of its focus on the economy as this is where the game is built upon. But how does it affect the player?

author: [AEP] MoonMonger
content:
I may be alone in this (and somewhat allergic to token lockups) but it would take more than voting rights and yield for me to lock up a volatile asset like POLIS. I think there needs to be some gameplay incentives sprinkled in. For example: a luck bonus depending on duration of lockup, crew morale boost, longer supply duration for ships, mining efficiency, NFT drops,  etc...

I think we need more benefits that don't take away from the economics (i.e. don't break the bank) but provide further incentives directly to gameplay (even mini-games) to further incentivize POLIS lockup.

Thanks

author: SuhailDebar | Star Atlas ModMod
content: Maybe...  There's no proof it was deliberately misleading but it sure as hell is misleading, having a major fact about how the emissions work as a footnote on a different page to the chart about emissions is very poor communication and misleading...  it will be interesting to see if it gets changed any quicker than the other misleading stuff in the white paper like the fact you can't stake atlas to earn polis...  I know things change, but if the white paper is out of date, then take it down or take out the out of date information or update it or put a health warning on it... people new to the project make their decisions to invest based on those documents, having inaccurate/misleading information in them will just be detrimental in the long run as people will feel that they have been deliberately misled even when that was not the intention...  I'm willing to believe it was not deliberate, as you say it could harm things but they do have something to gain... but that doesn't mean I'll just ignore it and not be on the look-out for other misleading information...  It's not changed anything for me, I'm still invested in ships and polis for the regional/faction dao, I never saw any value in SA dao whilst not decentralised and hope we get the promised AAA game in the future... It's just annoying the reality of staking was not made clearer in the economics paper...

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]

Dear Pedro aka @PG I BULK,

It was a busy day over here, but I finally had some time to go over the whole discussion from earlier once again.

I understand that by using the phrase "Your ignorance is not helping the project at all" I offended you, and I apologize for expressing myself that way.

Originally, I interpreted your message to Michael as a request to him to ignore all concerns raised over the weekend concerning the Polis DAO, and to not spend time on the publication he announced. But by re-reading your messages from earlier, I understand my interpretation was likely wrong, and you were -like you clarified- only trying to show Michael your support regarding the matter.

I stand corrected

author: [AEP] Dez [AEP]
content:
You touched on this briefly, but when can we expect to know more about the Internal Oversight and Controls over Insider Gameplay and Participation? 

You mentioned intent which isn't the point. I get that your team are good people and wouldn't intentionally do anything to harm the community, but I believe they'll have inherent edge over us (non-insiders). 

Specifically, Will we get to know things like:
- Getting to see the actual policy around Insider Participation in Gameplay
- Usernames/Wallet Addresses (Discord and In-Game) of Insiders who play as well as if they are in DAC's and/or hold leadership positions in those DAC's
- Data around Insider Polis Holdings and Transactions of Insiders (kinda like an SEC Form 4)
- A report of # of Insiders who participated in the Rebirth Event as well as future events

I'm not asking for restrictions, simply oversight and controls that monitor and enforce a fair environment for all. When any individual joins an organization or company there is an expectation that they would lose some autonomy/anonymity. I think we all would like more transparency in this area.

content: The current page has 39 people listed in game / chain development type roles, plus 3 partners - Hydra, SperaSoft & VertPaint...

author: Mjr_Eazy
content:
If we take those 39, plus the 68 from SperaSoft Michael has stated that's over 50% in development type roles, which is pretty good, in my experience - I built a team from 8 to 600 to deliver a $350 million dollar project - this is pretty good.  Optimum squad size is 7 +/-2, you then need to create tribes from that with tribe leaders and you really want an independent set of security team(s) - front-end, server, infrastructure etc, it's looking reasonable...
Plus all the other stuff you need to operate effectively what I affectionately call the "Worth something? overhead"...

content:
@Deleted User @CLUB | ℝ𝕦𝕤𝕥𝕚 
Yea, I think it was a quick answer that has had more clarification added in pieces over time. 

This is what I have gathered: 
Some ships are inherently Rare or Rarity for ships is based on number/density in the population of ships. Some some ships base level may or may not be Common. 

A newly minted ship could either have base level (Common) Modules/Components or none at all in a slot (like Airbike weapon), or the builder may create it with higher tier parts. 

The modules/components can be upgraded Common-Legendary which will provide boosts to aspects of a ship and increase performance, and maybe(Guess) this could promote a ship like an X4 up to higher tiers (I would imagine a cap for the ship itself though, like X4 can only get to Epic if all mods are Legendary). 

Consensus is that all Crew/modules/Components as part of GAO will come Base level. 
So maybe you could upgrade Components to get an Om from its base Epic rarity up to a Legendary or Anomaly eventually?

author: ROME | ENIGMA 42
content: I'd like to add a bit of clarity on what is happening behind the scenes. A LOT more than 2 of the ships are already modeled in 3D. Its all the extremely high poly details, animations, VFX, and the polished standards we are working towards that makes calling a ship "finished" take a while. We also have lots of devs working the showroom level/environment (also extremely detailed) not just the ships. I'm very confident once players are able to hop into the showroom, they will see why it took months to develop.

author: Mjr_Eazy
content: I wish I could just give you a backroom tour so you could see everything rapidly going on haha.  This would likely calm your frustrations regarding how long AAA development takes especially considering we started from just a concept early last year and we are building on UE5 (which is still early access) and Solana (which is beta).    The ship design and art pipeline is a well oiled machine. We are able to pump those out pretty quickly. We are also trying to round out our lineup across the FACs, a simple example is Busan doesn't have any released ships, but needs some.  The UE5 pipeline is much more labor intensive and the first few ship models have their growing pains as we establish our processes, metrics, solutions to issues as they arise. You have to account for actual physical metrics now, make sure things fit on landing pads as an example, players can walk inside/fit inside the interiors, make the meshes, account for weapon sizes and gimbaling, modules in the interiors, mock up VFX, animations and rigging (make sure there's no clipping), sound FX. I'm by no means an expert on this pipeline, but I'm just trying to paint a picture as to why it can take longer. This process gets faster over time after getting through the initial growing pains.

author: Mjr_Eazy
content: Yeah, I get it, first few will take more time and I appreciate your answers but even if we say there are only 5 people working on ships and it takes 3 months a ship that should get us 10-15 ships by launch of showroom.  That’s my concern, even if it’s only 2 people, 1% of the staff working on ships we should still have 4 or 5 by showroom.  I’ve not done it, maybe 3 months is too quick to fully model a ship inside and out, but that sounds like a helluva long time to me and with 75 ships by year end it’s a big back-log & a lot worse if it takes longer and I don’t see how this isn’t going to cause massive grief for the project.  There’s going to be a lot of disappointed people when they find out there’s only 2 ships on launch of showroom and it is going make things a lot worse when they hear it will take years to have them all available…

author: [Dark] gtl3Ustur
content: I believe the way it would work is that NFT add-ons/modules can be associated with ship NFTs as smart contracts.  It would also be easy to trade pieces of the ships or even recover them in the HRZ if they become flotsam.  Making them fungible would probably be only logical if you need to recover part of the ship if they get destroyed.  So depending on destruction you get a fraction of a ship.  But currently, them being fungible would be troublesome from a tracking and security perspective.  I'll have to look it up in the contracts and see what they are and maybe SA has already taken care of it.

content:
@[AEP] MoonMonger Hey man, to argue() with your suggestion, what we need is not to peg the atlas, but more usecase with gameplay. I think people get really stuck on the Atlas value right now, not thinking about the entire Crypto market uncertainty and falling pattern for the past few months.

The only way to get Atlas up is usecase, a reason for people to buy atlas instead of selling it. I believe the usecase will come as soon we get Score T1 and Cream modules, it'll help imo. We'll see. I dont expect the economy to thrive before we get lands and mining etc.

author: [AEP] Lingeke
content: Regarding Atlas: As long as there is more Atlas out there than assets you can purchase, the value will drop. It's exactly the same as with any Fiat currency. When a currency exceeds the value of the goods within the economy (seignorage capital) you get high inflation. Looking at the US dollar, it has lost 97-98% of it's value since implementation of the FED to stabalize it's value... Great effort. The utility of the currency and the value of the assets within the eco system will create value of the currency. At the moment, there is little utility and a constant influx of Atlas. I hope, further assets such as land will be sold in Atlas only. This will likely bring up the value quick and bind the capital. Once you have a large scale economy you can have a low scale inflation of currency to keep the prices stable and avoid deflation. Although deflation is not always bad. The whole wealth increase during the industrialisation happened with a constant deflation of the currencies. Heading off course here a bit...

author: [AEP] Bodhitree
content: If we create access nfts now we’re going to have one of 2 problems. content: I am purposely trying not to change any other parameters here. I just want the money to flow to the team (or DAO treasury) instead of to Discord

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: I can imagine the team might not want to sell an NFT with the express promise people buying these will have permanent access to the team via these channels, but that is fine. They can sell them for the equivalent of 3-6 months of boosting (Nitro costs) and only need to (well, more or less) guarantee that the system remains in effect for the same amount of time.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: These things can be SFT's, like the other items currently on the market, and never have to be converted to NFT's, making the costs associated with introducing these pretty low as well

author: [AEP] Bodhitree
content: I disagree. At this point in time the barrier to entry is low, it only costs a few dollars a month to get access. Since it is the only benefit, people know what they're buying. And as we have not hit the max booster ceiling yet, there is no reason to assume that creating a low-cost NFT which only serves the exact same purpose, would suddenly get many hundreds of additional people in tbh.  And if more people would suddenly buy one, that might not be so bad. The team could even make this sweeter by selling the NFT for ATLAS that flows into the DAO treasury (similar as R4).  As to total supply, they can mint an. "infinite" amount, mimicking the R4.  As the team seems happy with the current solution, I doubt they would suddenly want to switch to a model where people would suddenly need to pay a huge chunk of USDC/ATLAS to acquire access tbh.

author: [AEP] Lingeke
content:
My 2 cents:
There is no perfect solution. With the current setting you will get more and more people in who may not be fully commited to the game and get too many posts. Hence the chat was created.
If you take a high priced NFT such as the VIP badge you will miss out on several enthusiastic players. Not everyone who is into the game was early enough to get the posters and may not have the hundreds of $ to buy such a badge.
My proposal: use a combination of any badge plus level on the server (rank). Maybe level 10 or 12 and up.
Could require higher levels for lower badges. Not perfect either but more accessable and takes activity into account.

author: CLUB | Bazzo [CATS]
content: The whitepaper gave me the foundation from the start and they have followed it detail and in spirit.  They will continue to follow it with the DOA staking and game development.  No intervention is the healthiest thing for an economy.  Just know that there will be lows and highs on the rollercoaster of developing economy.  There are two developing economies here, the Crypto gaming economy and the Star Atlas economy.  Add in that the real economy is being put through an episode with world events and that is even more instability.  What's great to see is that the SA team is focused on the development of what they can control and not in playing economic god with the marketplace.  I respect this 100%

Not too sure about the positive effect of the "adjustments" done by the FED.
The buying power of the USD was quite stable for a very long period.
The FED was founded in 1913 to stabalize the value of the USD. I would think their accomplishment can be argued.

I welcome the thought of a 4% inflation of Atlas per year. This should be enough to fuel an ongoing growth with a sound monetary supply.
Any QE may result in a rise in prices, but not in value.

author: [Dark] gtl3Ustur
content: Question for anybody who knows  I'm wondering if Star Atlas allows or will allow user created content and if they will have a tool like VoxEdit in Sandbox. I understand if this will require raw materials and if SA and eventually the DAO approves and gets a cut of each NFT sold. This came up because I was talking to an architect friend who is vaguely familiar with crypto but is a space/manga/marvel fanatic and artist. He asked if he could draw and create ships for SA. My initial response was SA controls and has legal limitations in place for those. But afterwards I thought why not allow 3rd party creators.  Maybe not ships but eventually other things like clothes, skins, add ons. This has to happen eventually if they want a true decentralized metaverse. I am just wondering what their plan for this is. Sandbox is getting user traction because of user created content and tie-ups not just ship releases. I feel that and UE teases and walkthrus will get old in 2 years

author: [Dark] gtl3Ustur
content: He's got a job and we were just talking about it. It seemed to me to be an opportunity to get more people involved as users not just as employees. I frankly think this more as a user base growth strategy than just getting things out the door from SA perspective. I believe if they get the artist types into this then it will grow the entire economy.  I'm here for the gameplay and economics but there's more types out there. Imagine SA with its own space themed Nft marketplace. SA can get a cut and more people get involved.  Also in line the decentralized future. But if it's "maybe in the future" then that's good to know but feels like a wasted opportunity. Anyway thanks for the response.
